summary: The signed 32-bit result ceiling fixes decimal length at ten, so the complete two-digit search contains at most 2046 non-leading strings and cannot support unbounded asymptotic runtime scaling.

workload_bound.source_constraint: Every result must be at most 2^31 - 1 and may use at most two distinct decimal digits.

replacement_checks.evidence: At each of at most ten positions there are at most two digit choices, and excluding leading zero only lowers the sum below 2^1 + ... + 2^10 = 2046.

Authored cases cover zero-only input, duplicate digits, leading-zero exclusion, strict comparison with k, digit ordering, deeper breadth-first answers, divisibility, and exhaustion at the signed limit.
